mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate discount rates and lower borrowing costs, directly increasing the present value of future equity cash flows and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low-rate periods are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate falling below 6.0 percent signals labor market tightening that forces wage growth and boosts consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions higher and lifting equity prices.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45 percent of months where the unemployment rate crosses below the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, increasing revenue growth expectations for S&P 500 constituents and generating positive medium-term returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, supporting short-term equity momentum through improved corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 when the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent, or 5-day returns are negative in over 60 percent of strong industrial months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5 percent year-over-year triggers inflation expectations that force central bank tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and causing forward returns to decline.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where inflation exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold, invalidating the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer purchasing power, directly increasing corporate revenue streams and driving positive medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month rolling correlation between payroll growth and S&P 500 returns falls below 0.10, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of high-growth employment months.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing industrial production signals a shift toward lower-margin service sectors, compressing aggregate corporate profitability and triggering short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Periods of GDP-industrial production decoupling show positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations, refuting the margin compression hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that overwhelms liquidity effects, reducing economic output expectations and causing equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of tightening periods accompanied by falling industrial production, disproving the supply contraction mechanism.
